metadata.description: Single-click OpenClaw deployment — provisions an Ubuntu 22.04 VM, installs Node.js + OpenClaw via cloud-init, configures the chosen model provider and Telegram channel, exposes the agent gateway on port 18789. Total provisioning time: ~50–90 seconds before VM is ready, +30s for OpenClaw boot.
Short name (3-12 chars). Used as a prefix for all created resources. Lowercase letters and digits only.
Which LLM provider OpenClaw should route to. moonshot=Kimi K2.6, anthropic=Claude, openai=GPT, google=Gemini, deepseek=DeepSeek, xai=Grok.
API key for the chosen provider. Stored as a Linux env var on the VM (not echoed to logs).
Optional. Telegram BotFather token if you want OpenClaw to listen on Telegram. Leave blank to deploy without a channel — you can add one later via the agent gateway UI.
VM size. B2s (2 vCPU / 4 GB RAM) is the recommended baseline. Drop to B1ms for cheaper testing, bump to D2s_v5 for heavier skill loads.
Linux username for SSH. The deployment will not enable password auth — set sshPublicKey below to access the box.
Your SSH public key (paste the contents of ~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub or id_ed25519.pub). Required — password auth is disabled by default.
Azure region. Defaults to the resource group's region.

resources.comments: Public IP — DNS-named so the gateway URL is stable across restarts
resources.name: [variables('publicIpName')]

resources.comments: NSG — allow only SSH (22) and the OpenClaw gateway (18789). No 80/443; the gateway speaks its own protocol on 18789.
resources.name: [variables('nsgName')]

resources.comments: The VM. cloud-init runs OpenClaw bootstrap on first boot. Image: Canonical Ubuntu Server 22.04 LTS Gen2.
resources.name: [variables('vmName')]

osProfile.customData:
[base64(concat('#cloud-config
write_files:
  - path: /etc/openclaw/env
    permissions: ''0600''
    owner: root:root
    content: |
      OPENCLAW_PROVIDER=', parameters('modelProvider'), '
      OPENCLAW_MODEL_API_KEY=', parameters('modelApiKey'), '
      TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=', parameters('telegramBotToken'), '
      OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=18789
  - path: /usr/local/bin/openclaw-bootstrap.sh
    permissions: ''0755''
    owner: root:root
    content: |
      #!/bin/bash
      set -euo pipefail
      LOG=/var/log/openclaw-bootstrap.log
      exec > >(tee -a $LOG) 2>&1
      echo "[$(date)] OpenClaw bootstrap starting"
      apt-get update -y
      curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_20.x | bash -
      apt-get install -y nodejs git build-essential
      npm install -g openclaw@latest
      mkdir -p /opt/openclaw && chown ', parameters('adminUsername'), ':', parameters('adminUsername'), ' /opt/openclaw
      set -a; source /etc/openclaw/env; set +a
      sudo -u ', parameters('adminUsername'), ' --preserve-env env HOME=/home/', parameters('adminUsername'), ' bash -c "cd /opt/openclaw && openclaw onboard --non-interactive --provider $OPENCLAW_PROVIDER --gateway-port 18789 || true"
      cat > /etc/systemd/system/openclaw.service <<EOF
      [Unit]
      Description=OpenClaw agent gateway
      After=network-online.target
      Wants=network-online.target
      [Service]
      Type=simple
      User=', parameters('adminUsername'), '
      EnvironmentFile=/etc/openclaw/env
      WorkingDirectory=/opt/openclaw
      ExecStart=/usr/bin/openclaw start --gateway-port 18789
      Restart=on-failure
      RestartSec=5s
      [Install]
      WantedBy=multi-user.target
      EOF
      systemctl daemon-reload
      systemctl enable --now openclaw.service
      echo "[$(date)] OpenClaw bootstrap complete"
runcmd:
  - /usr/local/bin/openclaw-bootstrap.sh
'))]
